steps.title: Read the example after the semicolon
steps.content: After the blank, the passage explains that that The North Star printed a letter about easy fortunes in California and "inspired readers to relocate." The example is one of newspapers actively encouraging westward migration.

steps.title: Choose a verb that matches 'encouraged/inspired'
steps.content: The blank needs a verb meaning to spur or promote westward migration — something synonymous with "inspired."

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — stimulated: means to spur into action, which matches "inspired readers to relocate." ✓
  • Choice B — assigned: misuse — newspapers do not "assign" a migration; the word does not fit the encouraging-movement sense. ✗
  • Choice C — opposed: reverses the meaning — the example from The North Star shows promotion, not opposition. ✗
  • Choice D — disregarded: reverses the meaning — the newspapers paid active attention to migration rather than ignoring it. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
